perturbation.original_text: 4. This contract shall be for a term of one year commencing on the date first above written; thereafter, it shall automatically continue until terminated by either party upon not less than thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. This contract shall be for a term of one year commencing on the date first above written; thereafter, it shall continue only with the written agreement from both parties, upon not less than thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the contract 'automatically continues' unless terminated. The modified text states it 'continue only with the written agreement from both parties,' creating a contradiction. It's now unclear if the contract renews automatically, as initially described, or if explicit consent is required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. This contract shall be for a term of one year commencing on the date first above written; thereafter, it shall continue only with the written agreement from both parties, upon not less than thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the contract 'automatically continues' unless terminated, while the changed text requires 'written agreement from both parties' for the contract to continue. The contradicted text simply repeats the changed text, solidifying the contradiction. This creates a direct conflict, as the original automatic renewal is replaced with a requirement for explicit written agreement, leading to uncertainty about the contract's renewal terms. Therefore, there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text:
ITEM 120: DEFINITIONS
When used herein and in contracts making reference hereto, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:
"Consignee" shall mean the person to whose facilities a shipment is destined.
"Consignor" shall mean the person at whose facilities a shipment originates.
"Freight Bill" shall mean any receipt, manifest, bill of lading form or other document used to identify a shipment and evidence Carrier's receipt and delivery thereof.
"Month" shall mean a calendar month.
"Normal Business Hours" shall mean 8:00 a.m. to 5 p.m. local time on weekdays (Monday through Friday), holidays excluded.
"Shipment" shall mean a lot of freight tendered for transportation from one Consignor, at one point at one time for one Consignee at one destination and covered by one freight bill. Unless otherwise provided, charges will be assessed on each unit required to transport a Shipment at the applicable minimum weight or truckload minimum weight shown for each Unit used.
"Tractor" shall mean a truck tractor.
"Trailer" shall mean a tank semitrailer.
"Unit" shall mean a truck tractor-semitrailer combination motor vehicle.
"Vehicle" shall mean a Tractor or a Trailer.
perturbation.changed_text:
ITEM 120: DEFINITIONS
When used herein and in contracts making reference hereto, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:
"Consignee" shall mean the person to whose facilities a shipment is not necessarily destined.
"Consignor" shall mean the person at whose facilities a shipment originates.
"Freight Bill" shall mean any receipt, manifest, bill of lading form or other document used to identify a shipment and evidence Carrier's receipt and delivery thereof.
"Month" shall mean a calendar month.
"Normal Business Hours" shall mean 8:00 a.m. to 5 p.m. local time on weekdays (Monday through Friday), holidays excluded.
"Shipment" shall mean a lot of freight tendered for transportation from one Consignor, at one point at one time for one Consignee at one destination and covered by one freight bill. Unless otherwise provided, charges will be assessed on each unit required to transport a Shipment at the applicable minimum weight or truckload minimum weight shown for each Unit used.
"Tractor" shall mean a truck tractor.
"Trailer" shall mean a tank semitrailer.
"Unit" shall mean a truck tractor-semitrailer combination motor vehicle.
"Vehicle" shall mean a Tractor or a Trailer.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of "Consignee" to "the person to whose facilities a shipment is not necessarily destined," we create an in-text contradiction. Throughout the document, the term 'Consignee' is used with the standard understanding that they are the intended recipient of the shipment. This change undermines the clarity of obligations and responsibilities throughout the contract, especially in sections dealing with delivery and claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ITEM 240: PICKUP OR DELIVERY SERVICE Carrier's rates include, as to each shipment, one pick-up and one delivery at a place directly accessible to Carrier's unit at all points within the limits of the cities, towns, villages and other points from and to which the rates apply. The term "pick-up" as used herein means the service performed by Carrier in calling for and accepting a shipment. The term "delivery" as used herein means the service performed by Carrier in transporting and placing a unit as designated by the Consignee for unloading.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Consignee' clearly defines them as the destination of a shipment. By changing this definition to 'not necessarily destined,' the altered text directly contradicts Item 240, which states that 'delivery' means 'transporting and placing a unit as designated by the Consignee for unloading.' If the consignee is not necessarily the destination, the delivery instruction in Item 240 becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text:
ITEM 400: CLAIMS FOR CARGO LOSS OR DAMAGE
I. FILING OF CLAIMS
(a) Claims in writing required. A claim for loss, damage, injury, or delay to cargo shall not be paid by Carrier unless filed with Carrier in writing, as provided in subparagraph (b) below.
(b) Minimum filing requirements. A claim must be filed with Carrier within thirty (30) days from the date the shipment in question was delivered, and (i) contain facts sufficient to identify the shipment (or shipments) involved (ii) assert the grounds for Carrier's liability for alleged loss, damage, injury, or delay, and (iii) request payment of a specified or determinable amount of money.
(c) Documents not constituting claims. Bad order reports, appraisal reports of damage, notations of shortage or damage, or both, on freight bills, delivery receipts, or other documents, or inspection reports issued by Carrier or inspection agencies, whether the extent of loss or damage is indicated in dollars and cents or otherwise, shall, standing alone, not be sufficient to comply with the requirements of subparagraph (b) above.
(d) Claims filed for uncertain amounts. Whenever a claim is presented against Carrier for an uncertain amount, Carrier shall determine the conditions of the shipment involved at the time of delivery by it, if it was delivered, and shall ascertain as nearly as possible the extent, if any, of the loss or damage for which it may be responsible. It shall not, however, pay a claim under such circumstances unless and until a formal claim in writing for a specified or determinable amount of money shall have been filed in accordance with the provisions of subparagraph (b) above.
(e) Other claims. If investigation of a claim reveals that one or more other carriers have been presented with a similar claim arising out of or relating to the same shipment, Carrier shall communicate with each such other Carrier, and prior to any agreement entered into between or among them as to the proper disposition of such claim or claims, shall notify all claimants of the receipt of conflicting or overlapping claims and shall require further substantiation, on the part of each claimant of its title to the involved commodity or rights with respect to such claim.
perturbation.changed_text:
ITEM 400: CLAIMS FOR CARGO LOSS OR DAMAGE
I. FILING OF CLAIMS
(a) Claims in writing required. A claim for loss, damage, injury, or delay to cargo can be filed with Carrier either in writing or verbally, as provided in subparagraph (b) below.
(b) Minimum filing requirements. A claim must be filed with Carrier within thirty (30) days from the date the shipment in question was delivered, and (i) contain facts sufficient to identify the shipment (or shipments) involved (ii) assert the grounds for Carrier's liability for alleged loss, damage, injury, or delay, and (iii) request payment of a specified or determinable amount of money.
(c) Documents not constituting claims. Bad order reports, appraisal reports of damage, notations of shortage or damage, or both, on freight bills, delivery receipts, or other documents, or inspection reports issued by Carrier or inspection agencies, whether the extent of loss or damage is indicated in dollars and cents or otherwise, shall, standing alone, not be sufficient to comply with the requirements of subparagraph (b) above.
(d) Claims filed for uncertain amounts. Whenever a claim is presented against Carrier for an uncertain amount, Carrier shall determine the conditions of the shipment involved at the time of delivery by it, if it was delivered, and shall ascertain as nearly as possible the extent, if any, of the loss or damage for which it may be responsible. It shall not, however, pay a claim under such circumstances unless and until a formal claim in writing for a specified or determinable amount of money shall have been filed in accordance with the provisions of subparagraph (b) above.
(e) Other claims. If investigation of a claim reveals that one or more other carriers have been presented with a similar claim arising out of or relating to the same shipment, Carrier shall communicate with each such other Carrier, and prior to any agreement entered into between or among them as to the proper disposition of such claim or claims, shall notify all claimants of the receipt of conflicting or overlapping claims and shall require further substantiation, on the part of each claimant of its title to the involved commodity or rights with respect to such claim.
perturbation.explanation: The initial sentence of ITEM 400(I)(a) mandates that claims must be in writing. By changing this to allow verbal claims, it creates conflict with other sections of ITEM 400, particularly subparagraph (d), which presumes a written claim is required for uncertain amounts. This introduces uncertainty regarding the validity of claims made verbally.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
ITEM 400: CLAIMS FOR CARGO LOSS OR DAMAGE
I. FILING OF CLAIMS
(d) Claims filed for uncertain amounts. Whenever a claim is presented against Carrier for an uncertain amount, Carrier shall determine the conditions of the shipment involved at the time of delivery by it, if it was delivered, and shall ascertain as nearly as possible the extent, if any, of the loss or damage for which it may be responsible. It shall not, however, pay a claim under such circumstances unless and until a formal claim in writing for a specified or determinable amount of money shall have been filed in accordance with the provisions of subparagraph (b) above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in ITEM 400(I)(a) now states that claims for cargo loss or damage can be filed either in writing or verbally. This directly contradicts ITEM 400(I)(d), which explicitly requires 'a formal claim in writing' for uncertain amounts before Carrier pays a claim. The modified text introduces ambiguity and inconsistency regarding the acceptable form of a claim, directly conflicting with the requirement of a written claim in subparagraph (d).
